components.name: Observer stall detection
components.purpose: Keep liveness and stall interpretation entirely in the observer layer, separate from workflow safety enforcement.
planned_changes: Define stalled as 300 continuous seconds with no stdout/stderr activity and no step/status transition.
Use runner heartbeat/progress signals as inputs without moving workflow policy into observer code.
Preserve structured progress/status exposure needed for deterministic tests.

components.name: Canonical execution root contract
components.purpose: Make default-workflow step 04 the single source of truth for downstream execution cwd.
planned_changes: Retain `worktree_path` only as a temporary compatibility alias.
Remove all post-step-04 cwd/workspace fallback logic.
Require all downstream steps and helpers to consume `execution_root` only.

components.name: Execution root validation helper
components.purpose: Centralize path computation, canonicalization, trust checks, and safe worktree/branch setup.
planned_changes: Create a shared helper that computes and emits structured execution-root metadata.
Reject non-absolute, missing, non-writable, symlink-ambiguous, untrusted, or otherwise unsafe paths.
Create or reuse worktree/branch state safely and audit the resolved root and branch.

components.name: GitHub identity enforcement
components.purpose: Require exact authenticated GitHub identity match before any `gh` mutation is allowed.
planned_changes: Propagate `expected_gh_account` through orchestrator/workflow inputs and state.
Add a shared validator that resolves the authenticated `gh` login and enforces exact match.
Guard issue creation, PR creation, and all other in-scope GitHub mutation call sites.
Fail closed on unauthenticated, mismatched, or unverifiable identity.

implementation_order: Implement `amplifier-bundle/tools/setup_execution_root.py` to canonicalize and validate execution roots, reject wrapper tmp paths, enforce trusted absolute existing writable paths, and safely create or reuse worktree/branch state.
Update default-workflow step 04 to call `setup_execution_root.py` and emit structured `execution_root`, while preserving `worktree_path` only as a temporary compatibility alias.
Remove every post-step-04 cwd/workspace fallback path and make all downstream execution depend strictly on `execution_root`.
Update `smart-orchestrator.yaml` and `orch_helper.py` to propagate and consume `execution_root` consistently.
Implement `amplifier-bundle/tools/validate_gh_account.py` with exact-login enforcement, fail-closed behavior, redaction of sensitive auth output, and auditable structured output.
Thread `expected_gh_account` through smart-orchestrator and default-workflow and guard all in-scope `gh` mutation sites with the shared validator.
Add the pure stall-evaluation logic in `rust_runner_execution.py` and preserve the needed progress/status signals in `dev_intent_router.py`.

Add or update tests covering active-output, status-transition, threshold-boundary, and true-stall scenarios, plus execution-root enforcement, identity validation, and regressions proving removed fallback behavior stays removed.
Run the touched recipe and hook test suites and verify that no execution path can proceed via ambient cwd or unverifiable GitHub identity.
risks: Removing cwd fallbacks may expose hidden downstream assumptions previously masked by ambient working-directory behavior.
Execution-root trust rules may be either too strict or too permissive across developer environments unless ownership, writability, canonicalization, and trust semantics are defined carefully.
Temporary dual support for `execution_root` and `worktree_path` may create source-of-truth confusion if migration is incomplete or undocumented.
Exact `gh` account matching may break existing automation if `expected_gh_account` is not propagated to every mutation path in scope.
Stall detection may false-positive or false-negative if step/status transitions are not captured consistently at the observer boundary.
This plan is based on supplied design inputs because the referenced repository files were not present in the local workspace for re-verification.
security_considerations: Fail closed on invalid, ambiguous, or unverifiable execution roots and GitHub identities.
Never fall back to ambient cwd after workflow step 04.
Reject `/tmp/amplihack-rs-npx-wrapper*` and any symlink-ambiguous or untrusted execution root.
Require absolute, existing, writable, trusted paths before execution proceeds.
Use safe subprocess argument passing only; do not use shell-string interpolation in validation helpers.
Redact sensitive authentication output while still auditing resolved login, execution root, and branch decisions.
Ensure no GitHub mutation can proceed unless the authenticated `gh` login exactly matches `expected_gh_account`.
